title: ICE cotton rallies to 22 month-high on weaker dollar, drought worries
summary:
ICE cotton surged to a 22-month high, led by a weaker US dollar, firm crude oil and drought concerns in key US regions. 
The July 2026 contract hit its highest since July 2024. 
Strong trading volumes

sources.title: ICE cotton rallies to 22 month-high on weaker dollar, drought worries
sources.excerpt:
ICE cotton surged to a 22-month high, led by a weaker US dollar, firm crude oil and drought concerns in key US regions. 
The July 2026 contract hit its highest since July 2024. 
Strong trading volumes and rising synthetic fibre costs supported demand, while weather risks and macro factors kept market sentiment firmly bullish. 
Deliverable stocks remained unchanged, signalling tight supply conditions.

sources.title: Vietnam to streamline foreign trade procedures from May 1
sources.excerpt:
Vietnam will streamline foreign trade procedures from May 1 under a new decision aimed at cutting red tape and improving access to tariff preferences for businesses. 
The decision abolishes several administrative procedures, with a strong focus on simplifying certificates of origin (C/Os). 
Thirty six procedures were amended, mainly covering the issuance of preferential and non-preferential C/Os under FTAs.

sources.title: France’s inflation picks up pace in March 2026 amid energy prices
sources.excerpt:
France’s CPI rose 1 per cent MoM in March 2026, lifting YoY inflation to 1.7 per cent, driven by an energy surge. 
Petroleum prices jumped sharply, pushing energy up 8.9 per cent MoM and 7.4 per cent YoY. 
Core inflation edged to 1.1 per cent. 
Manufactured goods softened, while transport costs rebounded. 
HICP rose 2 per cent YoY, signalling broader inflation pressures.

title: Australian consumer confidence drops in April on rising living costs

Househol

sources.title: Australian consumer confidence drops in April on rising living costs
sources.excerpt:
Australia’s consumer sentiment plunged 12.5 per cent in April to 80.1, marking the sharpest fall since COVID, as rising fuel prices and interest rates triggered a fresh cost-of-living shock. 
Household finances and near-term expectations weakened significantly, while job loss fears hit a multi-year high. 
Housing sentiment remained subdued, and further rate hikes are expected as inflation persists.

sources.title: 2026 global growth to be 3.1% under ‘limited’ Iran war: IMF
sources.excerpt:
Assuming that the Iran war will have limited duration, intensity and scope, and the disruptions will fade by mid-2026, global growth is projected to be 3.1 per cent in 2026 and 3.2 per cent in 2027, according to the IMF’s World Economic Outlook. 
Global headline inflation is expected to rise to 4.4 per cent in 2026 and decline to 3.7 per cent in 2027, marking upward revisions for both years.

title: US CBP to soon launch electronic system for importers to claim refunds

Phase 1 will be limited to ce

sources.title: US CBP to soon launch electronic system for importers to claim refunds
sources.excerpt:
US Customs and Border Protection will launch on April 20 an electronic system that importers can use to claim tariff refunds authorised by court order or applicable law. 
Phase 1 will be limited to certain unliquidated entries and certain entries within 80 days of liquidation.
Refunds will be issued within 60-90 days of the Consolidated Administration and Processing of Entries declaration getting accepted.

Many local suppliers can provide inputs now against global orders.

sources.title: Ireland’s Primark boosts circular fashion with updated strategy

title: Hong Kong apparel imports stabilise after sharp dip last year

Casualwear led gains, with T-shirts and jerseys

sources.title: Hong Kong apparel imports stabilise after sharp dip last year
sources.excerpt:
Hong Kong’s apparel imports showed early stabilisation in January–February 2026, rising 0.7 per cent YoY to $946.49 million after a steep 2025 decline. 
Casualwear led gains, with T-shirts and jerseys increasing share, while structured wear softened. 
Despite this uptick, imports remain well below 2024 levels, reflecting cautious sourcing and weak global demand.
